sanskrit:
ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते।
सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यं च कूटस्थमचलं ध्रुवम्॥
सन्नियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धयः।
ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रताः॥
transliteration: ye tv akṣaram anirdeśyam avyaktaṃ paryupāsate | sarvatra-gam acintyaṃ ca kūṭa-stham acalaṃ dhruvam || sanniyamyendriya-grāmaṃ sarvatra sama-buddhayaḥ | te prāpnuvanti mām eva sarva-bhūta-hite ratāḥ ||
literal_meaning: They who worship the imperishable, indefinable, unmanifest, all-pervading, inconceivable, immovable, fixed—having restrained the entire group of senses, of equal mind everywhere, intent on the welfare of all beings—attain Me alone.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that seekers who pursue the impersonal Absolute through disciplined abstraction and universal compassion still arrive at the same inner wholeness; the path of formless meditation is slower and more austere, yet psychologically valid for those who need structure and distance before they can risk intimate relatedness.
psychological_state: Cognitive reassurance that avoids premature surrender; a defensive but necessary stage of abstract idealization before personal vulnerability feels safe.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out activist who has sworn off close relationships throws herself into anonymous online charity work, convinced that keeping people at arm’s length is the only way to stay effective and morally pure.
anchor_text: Seek the formless through disciplined senses and universal care; you still arrive at wholeness.
